coredata.dc:title: Novel hybrid supercapacitor based on ferrocenyl modified graphene quantum dot and polypyrrole nanocomposite
coredata.dc:creator: Rahimpour, Keshvar
Teimuri-Mofrad, Reza
coredata.dc:description: Graphene quantum dots (GQDs) have attracted great attention because of its unique properties which originate from its small dimensions. This work is the first report of ferrocenyl modified Graphene Quantum Dot (GQD-Fc) which performed as supercapacitor. In order to improve the electrode conductivity, the GQD-Fc physically mixed with Polypyrrole and obtained nanocomposite was used as electrode material. Benefiting from the high specific surface area of GQD as well as the presence of nitrogen and iron-rich contents in the synthesized nanocomposite, prepared electrodes reveal the good capacity behaviors include high mass specific capacitance of 284.01 F g−1 (2.5 A g−1), the 86% retention of initial capacitance after 5000 cycles in a three-electrode system along with the remarkable energy density (81.79 Wh kg−1) and power density (18.17 kW kg−1). Based on the obtained results, ferrocenyl modified GQD expected to be a competitive candidate for new kind of capacitive material as high-performance supercapacitor electrode.
coredata.dcterms:subject: Graphene quantum dot
Ferrocene
Supercapacitor

xocs:funding-list.xocs:funding-text: This research is supported by a research grant of the University of Tabriz (number SAD/6933-980401 ).

ce:caption.ce:simple-para: PL image of synthesized GQD. Inset shows photographs of GQD solution under 365 nm UV light.
Synergistic effects between GQD-Fc and PPy in synthesis nanocomposite.
FT-IR spectra of E-Fc, GQD, GQD-N3, GQD-Fc, PPy and GQD-Fc/PPy nanocomposite.
XRD patterns of GQD, GQD-Fc, PPy and GQD-Fc/PPy nanocomposite.
SEM graphs of a) GQD, b) GQD-Fc, and c) GQD-Fc/PPy nanocomposite.
EDX-map graphs for a) GQD, b) GQD-Fc, and c) GQD-Fc/PPy nanocomposite.
CV diagrams of GQD, GQD-Fc, PPy and GQD-Fc/PPy nanocomposite at a scan rate of 50 mV s−1 at 1 M Na2SO4 (Left). CV of the GQD-Fc/PPy nanocomposite at 50–200 mV s−1 at 1 M Na2SO4 (Right).

a) GCD curves of prepared electrodes at 2.5 A g−1 b) GCD curves of GQD-Fc/PPy nanocomposite at various current densities c) Specific capacitance of GQD, GQD-Fc, PPy and GQD-Fc/PPy nanocomposite at various current densities.
Csp of the GQD-Fc/PPy nanocomposite as a function of cycle number measured in 1 M Na2SO4 electrolyte vs. Ag/AgCl references electrode.
Relationship between energy density and power density at 1, 1.5, 2 and 2.5 A g−1 current densities.
Nyquist plots of GQD, GQD-Fc, and GQD-Fc/PPy nanocomposite.
Modification of GQD using ferrocenyl units.
Summary of the performance of GQD-Fc/PPy nanocomposite with reported supercapacitor materials.
The best fitting values for EIS data on equivalent circuit (EEC).

head.ce:title: Novel hybrid supercapacitor based on ferrocenyl modified graphene quantum dot and polypyrrole nanocomposite

ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: Department of Organic and Biochemistry, Faculty of Chemistry, University of Tabriz, Tabriz, Iran
ce:affiliation.ce:source-text: Department of Organic and Biochemistry, Faculty of Chemistry, University of Tabriz, Tabriz, Iran

ce:abstract-sec.ce:simple-para: Graphene quantum dots (GQDs) have attracted great attention because of its unique properties which originate from its small dimensions. This work is the first report of ferrocenyl modified Graphene Quantum Dot (GQD-Fc) which performed as supercapacitor. In order to improve the electrode conductivity, the GQD-Fc physically mixed with Polypyrrole and obtained nanocomposite was used as electrode material. Benefiting from the high specific surface area of GQD as well as the presence of nitrogen and iron-rich contents in the synthesized nanocomposite, prepared electrodes reveal the good capacity behaviors include high mass specific capacitance of 284.01 F g−1 (2.5 A g−1), the 86% retention of initial capacitance after 5000 cycles in a three-electrode system along with the remarkable energy density (81.79 Wh kg−1) and power density (18.17 kW kg−1). Based on the obtained results, ferrocenyl modified GQD expected to be a competitive candidate for new kind of capacitive material as high-performance supercapacitor electrode.

ce:section.ce:para: Quantum dot (QD) referred to a nanomaterial with less than 10 nm size in three dimensions. High surface atomic ratio, considerable optical and electronic properties along with good conductivity cased these materials can find a good position in supercapacitors (SC) and electrode materials science in a short time [1]. Graphene quantum dot (GQD) is ultrasmall nanographene which has attracted great deal of research in electrochemical fields specially supercapacitors due to its superior properties, unique structure, and good conductivity [2].

Nowadays supercapacitors have received extensive interest as high performance electrochemical energy sources. Generally, carbonaceous compounds include carbon fibers, carbon nanotubes, graphene oxide are capacitive supercapacitor materials and their storage mechanism is based on electrical double layer capacitance (EDLC) [3,4]; graphene quantum dot (GQD) is no exception [5]. The GQD provides the double-layer properties and electrochemical active sites. Furthermore, it can be improving the conductivity of electrode material [6].

Ferrocene and its derivatives with good redox properties generally used as captivating organometallic electron mediators [7]. Based on excellent redox properties of ferrocenyl compounds, their hybrids with carbonaceous materials are expected to be used as electrode materials in supercapacitors. On the other hand, conductive polymers (CPs) such as polyaniline (PANI), polythiophene (PTh) and polypyrrole (PPy) are an important family of materials which used in energy storage devices specially supercapacitors and batteries [8]. Relative ease of preparation [9], light weight [10], reversible redox activity [11], high capacitive energy density [12], scalable synthesis [13], ability to form thin films [14], excellent thermal and environmental stability [15] are some of the interesting properties of polypyrrole.

Here, in continue of our previous studies on electrochemical behaviors of ferrocenyl compounds [16–20]; we report the first application of ferrocene grafted GQD as a nano-supercapacitors (NSCs). Initially GQD was synthesized according to carbonization of citric acid method [21]. In order to ferrocene functionalization of GQD, triazole ring was formed via click reaction between ethynyl ferrocene and GQD-N3 [22]. Finally, GCD-Fc/polypyrrole was performed as electrode material and electrochemical properties of GCD-Fc/polypyrrole coated carbon paper was investigated using cyclic voltammetry (CV) and electrochemical impedance spectroscopy (EIS). Specific capacitance, energy density and power density of prepared nanocomposite was obtained from galvanostatic charge-discharge measurement (GCD). Based on obtained result, synthesized nanocomposite can be act as a suitable charge storage device.

All of chemicals [citric acid, sodium hydroxide, FeCl3·6H2O, ferrocene, acetyl chloride, sodium borohydride, aluminum chloride, sodium azide, dimethyl-formamide, dichloromethane, ascorbic acid (AA), CuSO4·5H2O, Na2SO4] were used without further purification. Pyrrole was distilled under reduced pressure before use.

(2-Formyl-1-chlorovinyl)ferrocene was prepared according to Ref. [23]. In summary: 4 mmol (0.6 g) of POCl3 was dissolved in 5 ml DMF and was added to solution of 1 mmol (0.2 g) of acetyl ferrocene [24] in 3 ml DMF at 0 °C. After stirring for 3 h (800 rpm), 1 ml of aqueous solution of sodium acetate was added to mixture under cooling and stirred for 12 h. Finally, the mixture was neaturalized with sodium bicarbonate and extracted with EtOAc. After solvent evaporation, the obtained deep purple solid is pure enough to use in next step without further purification. M.p. 76–77 °C, 1H NMR (CDCl3, ppm): 10.26 (1H, s, CHO), 6.40 (1H, s, =CH), 4.77–4.58 (4H, d, Cp), 4.26 (5H, s, Cp).

Ethynylferrocene was prepared using (2-formyl-1-chlorovinyl)ferrocene according to literature [25]. In summary: 5 ml of 0.5 M NaOH solution was added to 1 mmol (0.27 g) (2-formyl-1-chlorovinyl)ferrocene in 8 ml dioxane. After refluxed for 30 min, the mixture was cooled, poured in 30 g ice, and neutralized with HCl 2 M. The mixture was extracted with hexane, and after solvent evaporation purified using column chromatography (hexane as eluent). 0.80 mmol (0.17 g) of dark orange solid was obtained in 80% yield. M.p. 52–53 °C, 1H NMR (CDCl3, ppm): 4.56 (2H, s, Cp), 4.25–4.20 (7H, m, Cp), 2.72 (1H, s, HC≡).

3.86 g (0.014 mol) of FeCl3·6H2O was dissolved in 8 ml distilled water (DI) and slowly added to 1 ml (0.96 g, 0.014 mol) of freshly distilled pyrrole solution in 8 ml ethanol with continuous stirring (2000 rpm). In order to complete polymerization, the suspension was stirred for 24 h. Finally, the suspension was filtered and washed with acetone and distilled water. The obtained black precipitate of polypyrrole was dried for 24 h in dry desiccator.

In a ceramic crucible 2 g of citric acid (CA) was heated to 200 °C in furnace for 30 min. The obtained orange liquid was neutralized to pH 7.0 with 0.1 M NaOH solution. The solvent was removed by evaporation and the yellow solid was dried in 60 °C oven.

0.1 g of GQD and 0.1 g of NaN3 were ultrasonicated in 5 ml DMF for 30 min. Additionally, the mixture was magnetically stirred for 48 h in 40 °C. The suspension was centrifuged and precipitate was washed three times with DMF/H2O (5:1) and dried in dry desiccator.

12 mg of GQD-N3, 0.04 g (0.2 mmol) of ethynyl ferrocene, 0.04 g (0.2 mmol) of ascorbic acid and 0.03 g (0.2 mmol) of CuSO4·5H2O were ultrasonicated in 5 ml H2O/DMF (2:1) for 15 min. The orange solid was filtered off and washed with H2O and ethanol for removing of the remaining reagents. The obtained powder was dried in dry desiccator.

Aqueous suspension of GQD-Fc and PPy (1 mg ml−1), each one individually, were ultrasonicated for 30 min (Ultrasonic Power: 120 W, Frequency: 40 Hz) and then mixed together. The obtained mixture ultrasonicated for 30 min and magnetically stirred vigorously. After 72 h the mixture was centrifuged and the remaining black precipitate was dried in dry desiccator to gain the GQD-Fc/PPy nanocomposite.

Melting points were determined on a MEL-TEMP model 1202D and are uncorrected. The Bruker-Tensor 270 spectrometer used for recording FT-IR spectra. The Bruker FT-400 and 100 spectrometers used for collection of NMR data. The FESEM (MIRA3 TESCAN), EDX spectroscopy (MIRA3 TESCAN) and XRD analysis (PANalytica X pertPRO (Germany)) with a Cu-Kα (λ = 1.5406 Å) radiation source were used for analysis of surface morphology, elementary composition, phase analysis, and surface area, respectively.

Electrochemical measurements were performed by CV (potential range of voltammograms between −0.2 and 1.0 V vs. Ag/AgCl), GCD and EIS spectroscopy (EG&G PARSTAT 2263). The standard three electrode cell configuration: Ag/AgCl as the reference electrodes, platinum wire as an auxiliary electrode and 2 μl of a mixture of 80 wt % active materials (GQD, GQD-N3, PPy, GQD-Fc, and GQD-Fc/PPy), and 20 wt % carbon black, in NMP was coated on carbon paper as the working electrodes were used for all measurements. Aqueous solution of 1 M Na2SO4 was used as electrolyte at 25 °C.

In this research, we prepared a hybrid nanocomposite using ferrocenyl modified GQD, and polypyrrole. In order to preparation of modified GQD, we used ethynyl ferrocene. With respect to the expenditure and ease of access, the two-step synthesis, starting from acetylferrocene and preparation of (2-formyl-1-chlorovinyl)ferrocene as intermediate product, is one of the best and most successful method for the synthesis of ethynylferrocene [26]. Scheme 1 was illustrated the synthesis of ethynylferrocene, the excess amount of POCl3 was used for increasing the yield of reaction. (1-Chlorovinyl)ferrocene is an unstable by-product for this reaction, its production can be suppressed using excess amount of POCl3. In continues, elimination reaction was performed on (2-formyl-1-chlorovinyl)ferrocene as intermediate β-chloroaldehyde using aqueous NaOH in dioxane.

FT-IR spectra of ethynyl ferrocene (E-Fc), GQD, GQD-N3, GQD-Fc, PPy and GQD-Fc/PPy nanocomposite were shown in Fig. 4 . The broad peak at 3398 cm−1 in GQD spectra is attributed to –OH band as well as C–OH peak at 1087 and C–O–C (epoxy) peak at 1012 cm−1. The peak seen at 2068 cm−1 in GQD-N3 spectra was assigned to –N3 stretching vibration and approve the introduce of azide group to GQD structure. In GQD-Fc FT-IR spectra, the presence of specified Fe-Cp vibration at 486 cm−1 and disappear of –N3 and CC (2236 in E-Fc spectra) vibrations confirmed the click reaction between ethynyl ferrocene and GQD-N3. The correctness of PPy structure was supported by N–H at 3409 cm−1, CC at 1537 and 1457 cm−1 along with C–N vibration at 1301 cm−1. Finally, a set of characteristic peaks of PPy and GQD-Fc can be detected in GQD-Fc/PPy nanocomposite FT-IR spectra.

XRD patterns of GQD, GQD-Fc, PPy and GQD-Fc/PPy were depicted in Fig. 5 . The XRD analysis of GQD show a broad peak at 2θ = 25 (002) with 0.47 nm interlayer spacing, this is a well-known peak for producing of graphite-based structures and demonstrated the citric acid carbonization. Ferrocenyl modified GQD (GQD-Fc) show crystalline phase with characteristic ferrocenyl peaks at 2θ = 16.06 and 18.96 assigned to the corresponding (110) and (111) planes (ICDD PDF#29–1711) [27]. The diffraction peak of GQD was not detectable as the ferrocenyl crystals covered it. The XRD spectra reveals the amorphous nature of polypyrrole, broad peak at 2θ = 24 (104) with d-spacing of 0.37 nm was obtained for PPy powder. The XRD pattern of GQD-Fc/PPy nanocomposite shows all characteristic peaks of GQD-Fc and PPy (2θ = 15.96, 18.76 and 24) with small downshift for ferrocenyl peaks which can be arising from π-π interactions between PPy and ferrocenyl ring.

For surface morphology studies, scanning electron microscopy (FESEM) analysis were performed on GQD, GQD-Fc, and GQD-Fc/PPy nanocomposite (Fig. 6 ). The FESEM image of GQD showed sphere structure with the <10 nm particle size. The FESEM micrographs of the GQD-Fc showed grown sphere structure in the form of rod. It seems that trend of GQD to aggregation increases with introducing of ferrocene moieties to it surface, this can be because of π-π stacking between ferrocenyl ring and GQD. Furthermore, increasing of particle sizes is obvious. FESEM of GQD-Fc/PPy nanocomposite showed cumulus structure, created hydrogen binding and electrostatic interactions between polypyrrole and GQD-Fc increased the nanoparticles size and produced wrinkled surface pattern. The structure goes towards layering. The layered structure revealed the presence of countless nanochannels between the stacked layers. With increasing of miscibility of these blends, the electrical properties can be enhanced. obtained high surface area suggested applicability of obtained nanocomposite as electrode materials in supercapacitor.

Energy dispersive X-ray analysis (EDX) was used to understand the elemental composition and chemical characterization of the sample. EDX provides information on carbon, oxygen, nitrogen and iron content of our compounds. As can be seen in the Fig. 7 , the presence of Fe in EDX curves suggested the surface modification of GQD via covalent chemical bonding of Ferrocenyl groups. Elemental mapping images show the distribution of carbon and oxygen for GQD, additionally nitrogen and iron for GQD-Fc, and GQD-Fc/PPy nanocomposite, a relatively homogeneous distribution for all the four elements confirmed that the PPy was assembled onto the whole of the GQD-Fc to form an unmixed GQD-Fc/PPy nanocomposite.

For determination of the electrochemical performance of prepared compounds, cyclic voltammetry (CV) were recorded in a 1 M Na2SO4 electrolyte within a potential window of −0.2 to 1.0 V versus an Ag/AgCl reference electrode at a scan rate of 50 mV s−1. Fig. 8 depicts the typical voltammograms of the prepared electrodes with the scan rate of 50 mV s−1. The final nanocomposite shows rectangular image, indicating the capacitive behavior. The increasing in current density of GQD-Fc/PPy nanocomposite can be clearly seen by the incorporation of polypyrrole. Furthermore, increasing in current density of GQD with grafting of ferrocenyl moieties is obvious. The distortion from rectangular shape in GQD-Fc, and GQD-Fc/PPy nanocomposite originate from faradaic current of ferrocenyl groups. Addition of polypyrrole has improved the supercapacitive behavior of GQD-Fc and makes the CV curve more likely to a rectangular shape. The current density of the GQD-Fc/PPy nanocomposite is higher than others which originates from assimilation of two charge-storage mechanisms [faradaic (by ferrocene) and capacitive current of carbonaceous materials (by GQD)]. It is noteworthy that with increasing scan rate (50, 75, 100, 150, 200 mV s−1) the curves tend to become rectangular shape (Fig. 8); meaning the increasing of capacitive mechanism in compared with faradaic current. In high scan rates, the electrolyte ions do not have proper time to intercalate/de-intercalate into/from the GQD-Fc/PPy matrix.

For further investigation of prepared electrodes electrochemical performance, the galvanostatic charge–discharge curves were recorded at 2.5 A g−1 current density vs. Ag/AgCl reference electrode (Fig. 9 a). The specific capacitance (F g−1) can be calculated according to Eq. 1:
C
s p
=
I . Δ t
Δ V . m
where I, Δt, ΔV and m are the applied constant current (A), discharge time (s), the potential range of the charge–discharge (V) and mass of active material (g), respectively. For GQD-Fc/PPy nanocomposite the discharge time was longer than the others electrodes in galvanostatic charge–discharge curves. The calculated specific capacitance deduced from the Eq. 1 were 84.51, 129.72, 53.45 and 284.01 F g−1 for GQD, GQD-Fc, PPy and GQD-Fc/PPy nanocomposite, respectively. The specific capacitance of first electrode (GQD) was increased with the incorporation of ferrocene and polypyrrole. Fig. 9b shows the charge–discharge curves of the GQD-Fc/PPy hybrid supercapacitor at different current densities (1, 1.5, 2, 2.5 A g−1), as can be seen with decreasing of current density the discharge time was increased. This predictable trend for all of prepared electrodes was depicted in Fig. 9c. The significant distortion from linear relation between specific capacitance and current density in GQD-Fc and GQD-Fc/PPy is due to the ferrocenyl moieties on the GQD surface which could related to faradaic charge-storage mechanism of ferrocene.

The cycle stability of the GQD-Fc/PPy nanocomposites was investigated by galvanostatic charge- discharge measurements with a current of 2.5 A g−1 for 5000 cycles (Fig. 10 ). The 8.5% decay was observed for nanocomposite in available capacitance over 5000 cycles. The obtained results were confirmed the good long-term recycling capability of 86% for final nanocomposite.

The power density (P) and energy density of obtained electrodes were calculated using
P =
E
Δ t
and
E =
1 2
C
s p
( Δ v )
2
, respectively. In these equations, C is the gravimetric specific capacitance and V is the potential window of discharge process in GCD measurements. The maximum energy density of 81.79 Wh kg−1 and power density of 18 kW kg−1 were obtained for the fabricated electrodes in 2.5 A g−1 current density. The GQD-Fc/PPy supercapacitor has an energy density as high as 295.5 Wh kg−1 at a power density of 5 kW kg−1. The maximum power density can arrive at 18 kW kg−1 at energy density of 81.8 Wh kg−1 (Fig. 11 ). These results show that the GQD-Fc/PPy supercapacitor have both high power and energy densities. As listed in Table 1 , the energy density of GQD-Fc/PPy supercapacitor is much higher than that of other reported GQD-based supercapacitors. A comparison between the specific capacitances, power density and energy density of GQD-Fc/PPy nanocomposite with other GQD nanocomposites reported in literatures showed that the GQD-Fc/PPy can be a suitable candidate for supercapacitors application (Table 1).

For detailed investigation of as-prepared electrodes, EIS measurement was employed in the frequency range from 100 kHz to 10 mHz. Typical Nyquist plots of the GQD, GQD-Fc, and GQD-Fc/PPy nanocomposite in 1 M Na2SO4 are given in Fig. 12 . The Nyquist plot was divided into two regions, in the high frequency range the arc radius of Nyquist plot for GQD-Fc/PPy was smaller than GQD and GQD-Fc which indicate the lower charge-transfer resistance (Rct). This region is related to faradaic charge transfer and the low Rct is an ideal behavior for fast charge transfer. The inset plot in Fig. 12 show the low value of equivalent series resistance (ESR) for GQD-Fc/PPy (9.32 Ω) which indicated the improved charge carrier dynamics. The low frequency region (capacitive line) show a straight line.

Table 2 shows the results of fitting the EIS data using ZViewII software. The reduce in values of Rs and especially Rst for GQD-Fc/PPy nanocomposite are obvious. On the other hand, comparison of CPEdl-T values for GQD, GQD-Fc, and GQD-Fc/PPy show the higher interfacial capacitance for GQD-Fc/PPy nanocomposite which is in good agreement with results obtained from GCD analyses.

In this study, we proposed a facile method for modification of GQD with ethynylferrocne via click reaction which led to formation of ferrocenyl-based triazole ring on the surface of GQD. The structure of final nanocomposite was confirmed using FT-IR spectroscopy, FESEM, EDX and XRD. The superior electrochemical properties are observed for GQD-Fc/PPy nanocomposite in comparison to other GQD-based electrodes. The obtained results indicate that ferrocenyl modified GQD can function as a new kind of capacitive material for application as high-performance supercapacitors. The high gravimetric capacitance of 284.01 F g−1 at 2.5 A g−1 with excellent cycling stability (86% in 5000 cycle) was obtained for prepared electrode. Additionally, the energy density of 81.79 Wh kg−1 at the power density of 18.17 kW kg−1 demonstrate the good capability of prepared nanocomposite as electrode material for using in high performance supercapacitors.
Keshvar Rahimpour: Investigation, Software, Writing - original draft. Reza Teimuri-Mofrad: Project administration, Supervision, Writing - review & editing.

ce:conflict-of-interest.ce:para: There are no conflicts to declare.

ce:acknowledgment.ce:para: This research is supported by a research grant of the University of Tabriz (number SAD/6933-980401).
